data.title: Speak fluent AIO botting.
data.subtitle: The eleven terms every reseller uses in Discord — translated for newcomers.
data.author: StellarAIO

items.title: AIO
items.body: All-In-One. Software that automates checkout across many retailers from one dashboard.

items.title: Task
items.body: One configured purchase attempt — a profile + proxy + size + retailer URL.

items.title: Module
items.body: Retailer-specific logic. StellarAIO ships 70+ modules (Walmart, Best Buy, SNKRS, etc.).

items.title: Cook
items.body: A successful checkout. "Did you cook?" = "Did you get a confirmation email?"

items.title: Cooks group
items.body: Discord channel where members post confirmation emails as proof.

items.title: Proxy
items.body: A relay IP. Residential proxies look like real home internet to retailers.

data.title: Words you'll hear on drop day.

items.title: Profile group
items.body: A set of billing profiles, each tied to a different card and shipping address.

items.title: Captcha solver
items.body: External service (2Captcha, CapMonster) that solves CAPTCHAs for the bot.

items.title: Queue spot
items.body: Random position assigned by the retailer at drop time. You don't control it.

items.title: ATC
items.body: Add To Cart — the first irreversible step. If ATC fails, checkout never happens.

items.title: Restock
items.body: When a sold-out SKU goes back in stock mid-day. Bots monitor for this 24/7.

data.text: The reseller community has a vocabulary because every word saves time. Once you know what ATC, queue and module mean, the Discord stops being noise.

data.title: Forty more terms on the site.
data.subtitle: Bookmark the glossary page — built so newcomers stop having to ask in #general.
